message.content:
Adversaries may use obfuscation techniques to make files or information difficult to discover or analyze. This includes encrypting, encoding, or compressing payloads to evade detection across different platforms and networks. Common methods include Base64 encoding, XOR encryption, and using tools like ConfuserEx. Obfuscation can occur during Initial Access or later stages to avoid detection, often requiring user interaction to deobfuscate. Adversaries may also split payloads into benign-looking files or obfuscate commands executed via scripting interpreters.

The impact of this technique is significant as it hinders detection and analysis, allowing adversaries to maintain persistence and execute malicious activities undetected. Commonly exploited by various threat groups, obfuscation is used to hide malware, evade antivirus tools, and complicate forensic analysis.

Mitigation strategies include employing advanced threat detection tools capable of analyzing obfuscated code, using behavioral analysis to detect anomalies, and implementing strict email and download filtering to prevent the delivery of obfuscated payloads. Regular updates to antivirus and endpoint detection systems can also help in identifying and mitigating obfuscated threats.
